mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising supply-side pressures, leading to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the same period, signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1% increase in 3 months and CPIAUCSL < -1% decrease in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) over the past 6 months, combined with a 1% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a demand-side expansion that leads to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 > 2% increase in 6 months and UNRATE < -1% decrease in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a labor market expansion that leads to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 1% increase in 3 months and UNRATE < -0.5% decrease in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 6 months, combined with a 1% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the same period, signals a supply-side expansion that leads to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 2% increase in 6 months and INDPRO > 1% increase in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
